patch.value: MCP TOOL_SCHEMA_BASE additionalProperties:true silently swallows typo'd top-level args
All narrow tool schemas inherit additionalProperties:true; a typo like fullChangedField (missing s) is ignored with no error, so agents silently get default behavior.
Either additionalProperties:false with a documented allow-list of known top-level keys, or a server-side warning on unexpected keys; a typo-arg test triggers warning/error; existing tests pass.
All narrow tool schemas inherit additionalProperties:true; a typo like fullChangedField (missing s) is ignored with no error, so agents silently get default behavior.

value.author: mcp-platform-subagent
value.text: Warning mechanism (NON-BREAKING): kept additionalProperties:true. Precompute TOOL_DECLARED_KEYS = Map(toolName -> Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)) at module load. On tools/call, detectUnexpectedTopLevelKeys(name,args) returns warnings for any top-level arg not in the declared set; surfaced BOTH to stderr (console.error '[pm-mcp] ...') AND additively as structuredContent.warnings (only present when non-empty; result/content untouched). Cheap did-you-mean via existing levenshteinDistanceWithinLimit (OSA) with length-scaled limit (1..3). pm_run is EXEMPT (catch-all passthrough accepts arbitrary extension keys by design). Nothing rejected — agents still get default behavior, now with a signal.

value.author: mcp-platform-subagent
value.text: Gotcha for MCP unexpected-key detection: pm_run MUST be exempted. Its handler routes arbitrary non-reserved top-level args to extension/package actions via extensionOptionsFromArgs (catch-all passthrough by design), so flagging unexpected keys there would warn on every legit extension call. Narrow tools have no passthrough, so any undeclared top-level key there is a genuine typo — safe to warn. Also: TOOL_DECLARED_KEYS derives from inputSchema.properties which already includes the TOOL_SCHEMA_BASE keys (cwd/path/author) via objectSchema's spread, so cwd/path/author never false-positive. The smoke harness callTool reads structuredContent.result, so the additive warnings field does not disturb existing smoke assertions.

patch.value: Fixed in pm-at83. TOOL_SCHEMA_BASE keeps additionalProperties:true (non-breaking passthrough preserved), but the MCP boundary now precomputes each tool's declared top-level keys (TOOL_DECLARED_KEYS) and, on every tools/call, detects unexpected top-level keys — surfacing a warning BOTH to stderr ([pm-mcp] …) and additively in structuredContent.warnings (present only when non-empty; result/content untouched), with an OSA-Levenshtein did-you-mean hint. pm_run is exempt (it is the catch-all passthrough whose handler legitimately forwards arbitrary keys). Manually verified: pm_get with a typo'd `fullChangedField` produced the warning naming the declared args, while a clean call produced neither; covered by the new handshake test.
